commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainer review is needed because the PR is protected/draft and defines a new compatibility-sensitive Policy config/check-id contract rather than a narrow automation repair.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or secret-handling regression was found; the diff changes Policy code, tests, and docs without dependency, lockfile, workflow, package-script, or runtime enforcement changes. (sha=81a6dd4649920dd5cb281e119d22e8debe1a6cb5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Policy plugin config-conformance checks for ingress/channel posture, scoped-main ingress policy support, focused tests, and Policy docs updates.

PR surface: Source +2498, Tests +2255, Docs +342. Total +5095 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/config-conformance PR rather than a bug report. The review path is source/docs inspection plus the PR body's real CLI proof for failing and passing policy checks.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review is needed because the PR is protected/draft and defines a new compatibility-sensitive Policy config/check-id contract rather than a narrow automation repair.

target.title: Policy: add ingress channel conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds config-level Policy conformance checks for ingress/channel access posture:

```jsonc
{
  "ingress": {
    "session": {
      "requireDmScope": "per-channel-peer"
    },
    "channels": {
      "allowDmPolicies": ["pairing", "allowlist", "disabled"],
      "denyOpenGroups": true,
      "requireMentionInGroups": true
    }
  }
}
```

This stays in the config-conformance lane: it observes authored OpenClaw config, emits policy/doctor findings for drift, and does not add runtime enforcement.

The PR also lets `scopes.agents.<scopeName>.ingress` use the scoped-agent container from #85817. Current ingress evidence is default workspace/channel posture, so scoped ingress is intentionally limited to `agentIds` that normalize to `main` until non-default agent ingress evidence exists.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 232 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md docs/plan/policy-agent-scoped-overlays.md extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md docs/plan/policy-agent-scoped-overlays.md`
- `git diff --check refs/remotes/fork/policy-agent-scoped-design...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Policy can now express direct-message scope, channel DM policy, open-group denial, group mention-gate, and default scoped-ingress conformance requirements.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-85744`, rebased on the updated #85817 stack, using disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME`, disposable `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and disposable `policy.jsonc` files under `/tmp`.

Exact command run after this patch for each proof case:

```bash
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$proof/<case>.openclaw.json" \
  OPENCLAW_HOME="$proof/<case>-home" \
  OPENCLAW_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE=1 \
  node --import tsx src/entry.ts plugins enable policy

OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$proof/<case>.openclaw.json" \
  OPENCLAW_HOME="$proof/<case>-home" \
  OPENCLAW_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE=1 \
  node --import tsx src/entry.ts policy check --json
```

Failing ingress config used `session.dmScope=main`, Telegram `dmPolicy=open`, `groupPolicy=open`, and `groups.ops.requireMention=false` against policy requiring `per-channel-peer`, DM policies `pairing|allowlist|disabled`, no open groups, and mention gates in groups.

Failing output summary:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/ingress-dm-scope-unapproved",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/session/dmScope",
      "requirement": "oc://failing.policy.jsonc/ingress/session/requireDmScope"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/ingress-dm-policy-unapproved",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/channels/telegram/dmPolicy",
      "requirement": "oc://failing.policy.jsonc/ingress/channels/allowDmPolicies"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/ingress-open-groups-denied",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/channels/telegram/groupPolicy",
      "requirement": "oc://failing.policy.jsonc/ingress/channels/denyOpenGroups"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/ingress-group-mention-required",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/channels/telegram/groups/ops/requireMention",
      "requirement": "oc://failing.policy.jsonc/ingress/channels/requireMentionInGroups"
    }
  ]
}
```

Passing ingress config used `session.dmScope=per-channel-peer`, Telegram `dmPolicy=pairing`, `groupPolicy=disabled`, and the same ingress policy. It exited 0 and returned:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "findings": []
}
```

Observed result after fix: the real `openclaw policy check --json` path emitted the new ingress findings for a non-conforming config and accepted the conforming ingress config with no findings.

What was not tested: live channel runtime message delivery or enforcement; this PR intentionally adds Policy conformance/audit checks, not runtime enforcement.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
